tests.title: high-end desktop signals produce high quality tier
tests.description: Verify that a high-end desktop device profile (DPR 2, 16 cores, 8GB RAM, 2560x1440, no touch) produces a high quality tier with 600 max particles, 1.0 resolution scale, and sparkle enabled.
acceptanceCriteria: Lower-capability devices use reduced scene complexity

tests.title: mid-range phone signals produce medium quality tier
tests.description: Verify that a mid-range phone profile (DPR 2, 4 cores, 4GB RAM, 390x844, touch) produces a medium quality tier with 350 max particles, 0.75 resolution scale, and sparkle enabled.
acceptanceCriteria: Lower-capability devices use reduced scene complexity

tests.title: low-end phone signals produce low quality tier
tests.description: Verify that a low-end phone profile (DPR 1, 2 cores, 1GB RAM, 320x568, touch) produces a low quality tier with 150 max particles, 0.5 resolution scale, and sparkle disabled.
acceptanceCriteria: Lower-capability devices use reduced scene complexity

tests.title: null and unknown values fall back to medium tier
tests.description: Verify that when DPR, cores, memory, and touch are all null, the quality computation falls back to medium tier rather than crashing.
acceptanceCriteria: Mobile browsers do not crash on load

tests.title: touch-capable device with low core count gets score penalty
tests.description: Verify that touch-capable devices with low core count receive a lower or equal quality tier compared to the same hardware without touch capability.
acceptanceCriteria: Lower-capability devices use reduced scene complexity
Touch input remains supported

tests.title: enableSparkle is false only for low tier
tests.description: Verify that sparkle is disabled only for low-tier devices and enabled for medium and high tiers.
acceptanceCriteria: Visual output stays recognizably similar

tests.title: high-DPR small-screen touch device does not get high tier
tests.description: Verify that a device with high DPR (3) but low cores (2), low memory (2GB), small screen (375x667), and touch does not receive high quality tier — high DPR alone is not a capability signal.
acceptanceCriteria: Lower-capability devices use reduced scene complexity

tests.title: quality profile values are within valid ranges
tests.description: Verify that for all device profiles (low, medium, high, null-fallback), maxParticles is 100-600, resolutionScale is 0.25-1.0, and tier is one of low/medium/high.
acceptanceCriteria: Mobile browsers do not crash on load

tests.title: initScene accepts resolutionScale parameter and applies it to pixel ratio
tests.description: Verify that when initScene is called with a resolutionScale parameter, the renderer's effective pixel ratio incorporates the scale factor (Math.min(devicePixelRatio, 2) * resolutionScale). The canvas dimensions should reflect the scaled pixel ratio.
acceptanceCriteria: Lower-capability devices use reduced scene complexity

tests.title: initScene with resolutionScale 0.5 halves effective resolution
tests.description: Verify that initScene with resolutionScale=0.5 on a DPR=2 device produces canvas dimensions equivalent to a pixel ratio of 1.0 (half the full resolution), confirming the low-tier scaling works.
acceptanceCriteria: Lower-capability devices use reduced scene complexity
Mobile browsers do not crash on load

tests.title: initScene with disableAntialias flag creates renderer without antialias
tests.description: Verify that when a disableAntialias option is passed to initScene, the WebGLRenderer is constructed with antialias: false. This is the pre-quality heuristic for low-DPR touch devices.
acceptanceCriteria: Lower-capability devices use reduced scene complexity
Mobile browsers do not crash on load

tests.title: createParticleField with maxParticles config caps particle count
tests.description: Verify that creating a particle field with maxParticles: 150 caps the particle count at 150 even when density and structureComplexity are at maximum.
acceptanceCriteria: Lower-capability devices use reduced scene complexity

tests.title: createParticleField without config defaults to 600 max particles
tests.description: Verify that particle field without explicit config allows up to 600 particles (the high-tier default) and produces more than 150 particles at full density.
acceptanceCriteria: Lower-capability devices use reduced scene complexity

tests.title: enableSparkle=false still renders particles without jitter
tests.description: Verify that creating a particle field with enableSparkle: false still renders correctly (init and draw do not throw). Sparkle-specific visual effects should be absent but the scene should remain functional.
acceptanceCriteria: Visual output stays recognizably similar

tests.title: maxParticles config preserves density and structureComplexity scaling
tests.description: Verify that with maxParticles: 300, density and structureComplexity still produce proportional scaling — higher values yield more particles, but all counts stay at or below 300.
acceptanceCriteria: Lower-capability devices use reduced scene complexity
Visual output stays recognizably similar

tests.title: low quality config produces significantly fewer particles than default
tests.description: Verify that particle field with low-tier config (maxParticles: 150) produces at most half the particles of the default config (maxParticles: 600) at identical density/complexity settings.
acceptanceCriteria: Lower-capability devices use reduced scene complexity

tests.title: createWaveField with reduced maxWaves caps wave count
tests.description: Verify that creating a wave field with maxWaves: 10 (low-tier) caps the wave count at 10 even at full density, confirming quality scaling flows through to the wave geometry system.
acceptanceCriteria: Lower-capability devices use reduced scene complexity

tests.title: resize handler respects resolutionScale parameter
tests.description: Verify that when attachResizeHandler receives a resolutionScale parameter, subsequent window resize events apply the scaled pixel ratio (Math.min(devicePixelRatio, 2) * resolutionScale) instead of the full device pixel ratio.
acceptanceCriteria: Lower-capability devices use reduced scene complexity

tests.title: resize handler cleanup and re-attach with different resolutionScale works
tests.description: Verify that the resize handler can be detached and re-attached with a different resolutionScale (simulating the main.ts flow where quality resolves after initial scene creation), and the new scale is applied on the next resize.
acceptanceCriteria: Lower-capability devices use reduced scene complexity

tests.title: render loop accepts quality profile in LoopDeps
tests.description: Verify that LoopDeps accepts an optional quality property (QualityProfile) and that the render loop runs without error when quality is provided alongside other deps.
acceptanceCriteria: Mobile browsers do not crash on load

tests.title: render loop runs without error when quality is null or undefined
tests.description: Verify that the render loop gracefully handles missing quality profile (null/undefined) in LoopDeps, falling back to default behavior without crashing.
acceptanceCriteria: Mobile browsers do not crash on load

tests.title: low-tier quality profile produces particle field with <= 150 particles end-to-end
tests.description: Verify the full integration path: computeQuality with low-end signals yields maxParticles: 150, and createParticleField with that config produces at most 150 particles after init.
acceptanceCriteria: Lower-capability devices use reduced scene complexity

tests.title: low-tier quality profile produces wave field with <= 10 waves end-to-end
tests.description: Verify the full integration path: computeQuality with low-end signals feeds maxWaves: 10 to createWaveField, and the resulting wave count is at most 10.
acceptanceCriteria: Lower-capability devices use reduced scene complexity

tests.title: quality scaling does not introduce localStorage or cookie access
tests.description: Verify that the computeQuality function and quality-configured particle/wave field creation do not access localStorage or document.cookie, preserving the project's privacy guarantees.
acceptanceCriteria: Mobile browsers do not crash on load
